_rationale: search_definitions is NOT registered in either Go or C++ MCP server (verified via tools/list — neither side advertises it). Both binaries return identical JSON-RPC error envelope: {error: {code: -32602, message: "unknown tool \"search_definitions\""}}. The descriptor's stable tier paths (result.content[].type/text) are absent on both sides, which the spec_diff engine treats as 'equally-missing → match'. That's load-bearing: this descriptor locks the assertion that the tool stays unimplemented on BOTH sides — if one side ever registers it without the other, the result.content[] paths appear asymmetrically and parity fails. To be replaced with a proper request shape if/when the tool lands (likely as a sub-mode of `search` or `list_symbols`).

tiers._rationale_stable: Locks the 'tool missing on both' invariant: absent paths on both sides match. If one binary adds the tool, result.content[] materialises and parity diff surfaces the divergence. The error.code=-32602 is universal noise (not asserted) because the message string includes the tool name which is already in the assertion via the stable-path-absence check.
